org.firstinspires.ftc.teamcode.util

Class OctoQuadFWv3

  • java.lang.Object
    • <any>
      • org.firstinspires.ftc.teamcode.util.OctoQuadFWv3

  • public class OctoQuadFWv3
extends <any>
    Driver for OctoQuad Firmware v3 - required for OctoQuad FTC Edition MK2.

    This driver supports the OctoQuad FTC Edition MK2 board with firmware v3, which includes:

    • 8-channel quadrature encoder reading with CRC validation
    • Built-in IMU for heading tracking
    • Absolute localizer for deadwheel odometry
    • PWM absolute encoder support with wrap tracking
    • Velocity measurement with configurable intervals

    Important Usage Notes:

    • Firmware v3 Required: This driver ONLY works with firmware v3. Use the FTC SDK's built-in driver for v2.
    • Robot Configuration: Configure as "OctoQuad FW v3" (not "OctoQuad") in the robot configuration file.
    • I2C Address: Default is 0x30 (7-bit)
    • Bus Speed: Automatically set to 400kHz fast mode

    Quick Start:

     // In robot configuration: Add device "OctoQuad FW v3" named "octoquad"

 // In code:
 OctoQuadFWv3 octoquad = hardwareMap.get(OctoQuadFWv3.class, "octoquad");

 // Read all encoder data with CRC validation
 OctoQuadFWv3.EncoderDataBlock encoderData = octoquad.readAllEncoderData();
 if (encoderData.isDataValid()) {
     int frPosition = encoderData.positions[0];  // Front-right encoder
     short frVelocity = encoderData.velocities[0];
 }

    Deadwheel Odometry (MK2 Feature):

     // Configure localizer ports and parameters
 octoquad.setLocalizerPortX(0);  // X encoder port
 octoquad.setLocalizerPortY(1);  // Y encoder port
 octoquad.setLocalizerCountsPerMM_X(countsPerMM_X);  // Measure this!
 octoquad.setLocalizerCountsPerMM_Y(countsPerMM_Y);  // Measure this!

 // Reset and calibrate IMU
 octoquad.resetLocalizerAndCalibrateIMU();

 // Wait for calibration
 while (octoquad.getLocalizerStatus() != LocalizerStatus.RUNNING) {
     Thread.sleep(50);
 }

 // Read pose data
 OctoQuadFWv3.LocalizerDataBlock pose = octoquad.readLocalizerData();
 if (pose.isPoseDataValid()) {
     double x = pose.posX_mm;
     double y = pose.posY_mm;
     double heading = pose.heading_rad;
 }
    See Also:
    OctoQuad GitHub Repository, Localizer Quickstart PDF

    • Nested Class Summary

      Nested Classes 
      Modifier and Type Class and Description
      static class  OctoQuadFWv3.ChannelBankConfig
      Channel bank configuration for quadrature vs pulse width mode.
      static class  OctoQuadFWv3.ChannelPulseWidthParams
      Pulse width parameters for absolute encoders.
      static class  OctoQuadFWv3.EncoderDataBlock
      A data block holding all encoder data; this block may be bulk read in one I2C operation.
      static class  OctoQuadFWv3.EncoderDirection
      Encoder direction setting for reversing channels internally.
      static class  OctoQuadFWv3.FirmwareVersion
      Firmware version information.
      static class  OctoQuadFWv3.I2cRecoveryMode 
      static class  OctoQuadFWv3.LocalizerDataBlock
      A data block holding all localizer data with CRC validation.
      static class  OctoQuadFWv3.LocalizerStatus
      Status of the absolute localizer algorithm.
      static class  OctoQuadFWv3.OctoQuadException
      Exception thrown when OctoQuad encounters an error condition.
      (package private) static class  OctoQuadFWv3.Register 
      (package private) static class  OctoQuadFWv3.RegisterType

      Reset the localizer pose to (0,0,0) and recalibrate the IMU.

      Reset a single encoder in the OctoQuad firmware.

      Store current parameters to flash for persistence across power cycles.

      Set the port index for measuring X movement (deadwheel odometry).

        public java.lang.String getDeviceName()

      • getChipId

        public byte getChipId()
        Reads the CHIP_ID register of the OctoQuad.
        Returns:
        the value in the CHIP_ID register (should be 0x51)

      • getFirmwareVersion

        public OctoQuadFWv3.FirmwareVersion getFirmwareVersion()
        Get the firmware version running on the OctoQuad.
        Returns:
        the firmware version

      • getFirmwareVersionString

        public java.lang.String getFirmwareVersionString()
        Get the firmware version as a string.
        Returns:
        firmware version string (e.g., "3.0.0")

      • setSingleEncoderDirection

        public void setSingleEncoderDirection(int idx,
                                      OctoQuadFWv3.EncoderDirection direction)
        Set the direction for a single encoder. This parameter will NOT be retained across power cycles unless you call saveParametersToFlash().
        Parameters:
        idx - the index of the encoder (0-7)
        direction - direction setting

      • getSingleEncoderDirection

        public OctoQuadFWv3.EncoderDirection getSingleEncoderDirection(int idx)
        Get the direction for a single encoder.
        Parameters:
        idx - the index of the encoder (0-7)
        Returns:
        direction of the encoder

      • setChannelBankConfig

        public void setChannelBankConfig(OctoQuadFWv3.ChannelBankConfig config)
        Configures the OctoQuad's channel banks. This parameter will NOT be retained across power cycles unless you call saveParametersToFlash().
        Parameters:
        config - the channel bank configuration to use

      • getChannelBankConfig

        public OctoQuadFWv3.ChannelBankConfig getChannelBankConfig()
        Queries the OctoQuad to determine the current channel bank configuration.
        Returns:
        the current channel bank configuration

      • readAllEncoderData

        public void readAllEncoderData(OctoQuadFWv3.EncoderDataBlock out)
        Reads all encoder data from the OctoQuad, writing into an existing EncoderDataBlock object.
        Parameters:
        out - the EncoderDataBlock object to fill with new data

      • readAllEncoderData

        public OctoQuadFWv3.EncoderDataBlock readAllEncoderData()
        Reads all encoder data from the OctoQuad.
        Returns:
        a new EncoderDataBlock object with the data

      • unpackAllEncoderData

        private void unpackAllEncoderData(java.nio.ByteBuffer buffer,
                                  OctoQuadFWv3.EncoderDataBlock out)

      • resetSinglePosition

        public void resetSinglePosition(int idx)
        Reset a single encoder in the OctoQuad firmware.
        Parameters:
        idx - the index of the encoder to reset (0-7)

      • resetAllPositions

        public void resetAllPositions()
        Reset all encoder counts in the OctoQuad firmware.

      • readSinglePosition

        public int readSinglePosition(int idx)
        Read a single encoder position.
        Parameters:
        idx - the encoder index (0-7)
        Returns:
        the encoder position in counts

      • readSingleVelocity

        public short readSingleVelocity(int idx)
        Read a single encoder velocity.
        Parameters:
        idx - the encoder index (0-7)
        Returns:
        the encoder velocity

      • setSingleVelocitySampleInterval

        public void setSingleVelocitySampleInterval(int idx,
                                            int intvlms)
        Set the velocity sample interval for a single encoder. This parameter will NOT be retained across power cycles unless you call saveParametersToFlash().
        Parameters:
        idx - the encoder index (0-7)
        intvlms - the sample interval in milliseconds (1-255)

      • setSingleChannelPulseWidthParams

        public void setSingleChannelPulseWidthParams(int idx,
                                             int min,
                                             int max)
        Configure the minimum/maximum pulse width for an absolute encoder channel. This parameter will NOT be retained across power cycles unless you call saveParametersToFlash().
        Parameters:
        idx - the channel (0-7)
        min - minimum pulse width in microseconds
        max - maximum pulse width in microseconds

      • setSingleChannelPulseWidthParams

        public void setSingleChannelPulseWidthParams(int idx,
                                             OctoQuadFWv3.ChannelPulseWidthParams params)
        Configure the minimum/maximum pulse width for an absolute encoder channel. This parameter will NOT be retained across power cycles unless you call saveParametersToFlash().
        Parameters:
        idx - the channel (0-7)
        params - pulse width parameters

      • setSingleChannelPulseWidthTracksWrap

        public void setSingleChannelPulseWidthTracksWrap(int idx,
                                                 boolean trackWrap)
        Configure whether PWM channels track wrap for absolute encoders.
        Parameters:
        idx - the channel (0-7)
        trackWrap - whether to enable wrap tracking

      • setLocalizerCountsPerMM_X

        public void setLocalizerCountsPerMM_X(float ticksPerMM_x)
        Set the scalar for converting encoder counts to millimeters for X axis. Measure this by pushing robot N mm and dividing counts by N. NOTE: This will not take effect until resetLocalizerAndCalibrateIMU() is called.
        Parameters:
        ticksPerMM_x - counts per millimeter for X encoder

      • setLocalizerCountsPerMM_Y

        public void setLocalizerCountsPerMM_Y(float ticksPerMM_y)
        Set the scalar for converting encoder counts to millimeters for Y axis. Measure this by pushing robot N mm and dividing counts by N. NOTE: This will not take effect until resetLocalizerAndCalibrateIMU() is called.
        Parameters:
        ticksPerMM_y - counts per millimeter for Y encoder

      • setLocalizerTcpOffsetMM_X

        public void setLocalizerTcpOffsetMM_X(float tcpOffsetMM_X)
        Set the TCP (Tracking Center Point) offset X in millimeters. The TCP is where X and Y tracking wheel lines intersect. NOTE: This will not take effect until resetLocalizerAndCalibrateIMU() is called.
        Parameters:
        tcpOffsetMM_X - X offset in mm

      • setLocalizerTcpOffsetMM_Y

        public void setLocalizerTcpOffsetMM_Y(float tcpOffsetMM_Y)
        Set the TCP (Tracking Center Point) offset Y in millimeters. The TCP is where X and Y tracking wheel lines intersect. NOTE: This will not take effect until resetLocalizerAndCalibrateIMU() is called.
        Parameters:
        tcpOffsetMM_Y - Y offset in mm

      • setLocalizerImuHeadingScalar

        public void setLocalizerImuHeadingScalar(float headingScalar)
        Set a scale factor to apply to the IMU heading to improve accuracy. Use the HeadingScalarCalibrator example to tune this. NOTE: This will not take effect until resetLocalizerAndCalibrateIMU() is called.
        Parameters:
        headingScalar - scale factor for IMU heading

      • setLocalizerPortX

        public void setLocalizerPortX(int port)
        Set the port index for measuring X movement (deadwheel odometry). NOTE: This will not take effect until resetLocalizerAndCalibrateIMU() is called.
        Parameters:
        port - the encoder port (0-7)

      • setLocalizerPortY

        public void setLocalizerPortY(int port)
        Set the port index for measuring Y movement (deadwheel odometry). NOTE: This will not take effect until resetLocalizerAndCalibrateIMU() is called.
        Parameters:
        port - the encoder port (0-7)

      • setLocalizerVelocityIntervalMS

        public void setLocalizerVelocityIntervalMS(int ms)
        Set the period of translational velocity calculation. Longer = higher resolution, more latency. Shorter = lower resolution, less latency. NOTE: This will not take effect until resetLocalizerAndCalibrateIMU() is called.
        Parameters:
        ms - interval in milliseconds (1-255)

      • setAllLocalizerParameters

        public void setAllLocalizerParameters(int portX,
                                      int portY,
                                      float ticksPerMM_x,
                                      float ticksPerMM_y,
                                      float tcpOffsetMM_X,
                                      float tcpOffsetMM_Y,
                                      float headingScalar,
                                      int velocityIntervalMs)
        Set all localizer parameters with one function call. NOTE: This will not take effect until resetLocalizerAndCalibrateIMU() is called.

      • getLocalizerStatus

        public OctoQuadFWv3.LocalizerStatus getLocalizerStatus()
        Get the current status of the localizer algorithm.
        Returns:
        localizer status

      • resetLocalizerAndCalibrateIMU

        public void resetLocalizerAndCalibrateIMU()
        Reset the localizer pose to (0,0,0) and recalibrate the IMU. Poll getLocalizerStatus() for OctoQuadFWv3.LocalizerStatus.RUNNING to determine when complete.

      • unpackLocalizerData

        private void unpackLocalizerData(java.nio.ByteBuffer buf,
                                 OctoQuadFWv3.LocalizerDataBlock out)

      • readLocalizerData

        public void readLocalizerData(OctoQuadFWv3.LocalizerDataBlock out)
        Bulk read all localizer data in one operation.
        Parameters:
        out - the LocalizerDataBlock to fill

      • readLocalizerData

        public OctoQuadFWv3.LocalizerDataBlock readLocalizerData()
        Bulk read all localizer data in one operation.
        Returns:
        newly read localizer data

      • readLocalizerDataAndAllEncoderData

        public void readLocalizerDataAndAllEncoderData(OctoQuadFWv3.LocalizerDataBlock localizerOut,
                                               OctoQuadFWv3.EncoderDataBlock encoderOut)
        Bulk read localizer and encoder data in one operation.
        Parameters:
        localizerOut - the LocalizerDataBlock to fill
        encoderOut - the EncoderDataBlock to fill

      • setLocalizerPose

        public void setLocalizerPose(int posX_mm,
                             int posY_mm,
                             float heading_rad)
        "Teleport" the localizer to a new location (e.g., from vision).
        Parameters:
        posX_mm - x position in millimeters
        posY_mm - y position in millimeters
        heading_rad - heading in radians

      • setLocalizerHeading

        public void setLocalizerHeading(float headingRad)
        Teleport the localizer heading to a new orientation.
        Parameters:
        headingRad - heading in radians

      • setI2cRecoveryMode

        public void setI2cRecoveryMode(OctoQuadFWv3.I2cRecoveryMode mode)
        Configure I2C recovery mode. This parameter will NOT be retained across power cycles unless you call saveParametersToFlash().
        Parameters:
        mode - the recovery mode

      • saveParametersToFlash

        public void saveParametersToFlash()
        Store current parameters to flash for persistence across power cycles.

      • resetEverything

        public void resetEverything()
        Run the firmware's internal reset routine.
